content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

April 8, 1996

Sheryl Z. White
Statecraft, Inc.
6540 Lusk Blvd., Ste. C-106
San Diego, CA 92121

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-96-048

Dear Ms. White:

You have requested formal advice and a written opinion
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").1/ Section 83114 provides that any person may request
an opinion or written advice from the Commission with respect to
his or her own duties under the Act. Regulation 18329(b) states
that formal written advice may be requested in writing by any
person whose duties under the Act are in question or by that
person's authorized representative. Regulation 18329(c) provides
that other persons may request informal assistance concerning
provisions of the Act.2/

Your inquiry is general in nature and you have not indicated
that you are seeking advice as the authorized representative of a
person whose duties under the Act are in question. Therefore, we
must treat your letter as a request for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).

QUESTIONS

You have posed a series of questions concerning the
application of Regulation 18524(d), which allows candidates to

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

[illegible] Street, [illegible] Francisco, California [illegible] (916) 322-5660

Sheryl Z. White
Our File No. I-96-048
Page 2


establish one petty cash fund at each campaign office.
Specifically, you have asked where officeholders and candidates
may establish petty cash funds under various circumstances. Due
to the nature of your request, we we will limit our response to a
general explanation of the Act's requirements.3/

DISCUSSION

Section 85201 was added to the Act by the enactment of
Proposition 73 in the June 1988 primary election. It requires a
candidate to establish one campaign bank account for each specific
candidacy, to deposit all contributions for that candidacy in the
campaign bank account, and to make all campaign expenditures from
that account.

Section 85201 raised concerns about the ability of candidates
to invest campaign funds in interest-bearing accounts, to use
credit cards to charge campaign expenses, and to make small cash
expenditures. The Commission adopted Regulation 18524 to address
those concerns, which states, in part:

* * *

(a) The candidate·may establish, for each
campaign bank account, one petty cash fund at each
campaign office. No more than $100 shall be
deposited in a petty cash fund at any time. No

___________________________
3/' Regulation 18329(c)(4) provides that the Commission may
decline to provide informal assistance or may limit such
assistance to the explanation, in general terms, of the
requirement of the Act in any of the following circumstances:

(A) Assistance or advice is being sought regarding past
conduct, unless the advice or assistance sought is related to
possible amendment of previous reports filed by the person
requesting the advice.
(B) The requestor is seeking advice anonymously.
(C) Assistance or advice is being sought regarding the
duties of another person and the requestor does not appear to
be authorized to make the request as the person's
representative, or does not provide the identity of the
person on whose behalf the assistance is being sought.
(D) The question presented is purely hypothetical.
(E) The question presented is too complex or is
otherwise inappropriate for resolution by informal assistance
and should be resolved by formal written advice or an
Opinion.
(F) The facts presented are insufficient or too vague
to render specific informal assistance or do not appear to
present a question under the Act.
(G) Rendering informal assistance would be
inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest.

Sheryl Z. White
Our File No. I-96-048
Page 3


expenditure totaling $100 or more shall be made
from a petty cash fund. Expenditures of less than
$100 from a petty cash fund shall be deemed to be
expenditures from the campaign bank account. All
expenditures from a petty cash fund shall be for
expenses associated with the candidate's election
to the specific elective office designated in the
statement of intention or expenses associated with
holding that office.

Regulation 18524(d).

The regulation does not define the term "campaign office."
In a memorandum to the Commission dated November 10, 1988, the
Commission staff stated that "the petty cash account is intended
only for small purchases which are not crucial to the functioning
of the campaign, but are a practical necessity. For example, the
campaign might purchase donuts or emergency stationery supplies
with petty cash...."

The Commission's intent in adopting Regulation 18524 was to
allow for establishment of a petty cash fund at the campaign
headquarters. For example, a statewide candidate with campaign
offices in different locations around the state could maintain a
petty cash fund at each office. On the other hand, a local
candidate with no official campaign headquarters could maintain
one petty cash fund at the address from which the campaign is run,
which could be the candidate's home or place of business, or
possibly the treasurer's headquarters.

However, the regulation was not intended to allow the
candidate, the treasurer, and the campaign consultant each to
maintain a petty cash fund at his or her home and/or office. In
addition, although elected officeholders are prohibited from using
public resources for campaign purposes, an officeholder who has no
other location for maintaining a petty cash fund would not be
prohibited from maintaining a petty cash fund in his or her
government office to cover miscellaneous officeholder expenses.

The Commission staff is reviewing Regulation 18524 to
consider drafting clarifying amendments. If you have questions
concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
